hyperedges.label: Retry / Resilience Layer across LLM, Zep, and HTTP
i18n / Locale Pipeline (backend locale util + frontend Accept-Language header)
GraphPanel Shared Across All Step Views
Step2 Async Preparation Subsystem (Poll + Profile + Config)
Python Backend Core Stack (Flask + OpenAI + Zep)
Report fd139afc5936 Section Cluster
hyperedges.note: All three section files are parts of the same simulation report (report_fd139afc5936) covering financial context, sales friction, and competitive saturation for the Saltillo real estate digital market.

hyperedges.label: Saltillo Market Segments
hyperedges.note: Three distinct segments compose the Saltillo real estate market target audience: boutique agencies (40%), established agencies (35%), independent advisors (25%).

hyperedges.label: Large PDF Document Cluster (~5.7MB)
hyperedges.note: Six PDFs across different project folders share the same ~5.7MB file size, suggesting a common document template or type used across multiple simulation projects.

hyperedges.label: Small PDF Document Cluster (~21.5KB)
hyperedges.note: Six PDFs across different project folders share the same ~21.5KB file size, suggesting a common lightweight document (e.g., summary sheet or form) used across simulation projects.

hyperedges.label: Medium PDF Document Cluster (~524.4KB)
hyperedges.note: Three PDFs across different project folders share the same ~524.4KB file size, suggesting a common document type used across a subset of simulation projects.

hyperedges.label: Homepty Swarm Frontend to Report Pipeline
hyperedges.note: The Homepty Swarm SPA (entry point index.html) is the system that runs simulations and produces reports like report_fd139afc5936 analyzing markets like Saltillo.

nodes.label: Zep Graph as Persistent Memory Store
nodes.norm_label: zep graph as persistent memory store

nodes.label: IPC via File System Polling Pattern
nodes.norm_label: ipc via file system polling pattern

nodes.label: 5-Step Simulation Workflow Design
nodes.rationale: The application follows a strict 5-step workflow: (1) Graph Build, (2) Env Setup, (3) Simulation Run, (4) Report Generation, (5) Deep Interaction. Each step has a dedicated view and component. This design isolates concerns, allows per-step navigation, and enables resumable sessions.

nodes.label: Polling-based Async Progress Pattern
nodes.rationale: Preparation, config generation, and simulation run status are all tracked via periodic polling (setInterval) rather than WebSockets. This simplifies backend complexity at the cost of slight latency. Multiple timers (pollTimer, profilesTimer, configTimer) are used in Step2 to independently track different async operations.

nodes.label: section_01.md
nodes.summary: Financial context and market opportunity analysis for the Saltillo real estate digital market. Analyzes viability of 180-web goal, identifies three market segments (boutique agencies 40%, established agencies 35%, independent advisors 25%) with different decision timelines, projects a 6-week critical conversion window, and notes seasonal slowdown risk in month 3.

nodes.label: section_02.md
nodes.summary: Advisor behavior patterns and sales friction analysis. Maps 4 critical funnel drop-off points: price objection (60%), reference delay (45%), decision delay (35%), internal competition (20%). Identifies negotiation differences between independent advisors and established agencies. Documents senior advisor tech resistance (40% of market). Notes abandonment peaks at days 10-15.

nodes.label: section_03.md
nodes.summary: Market saturation trends and competitive response analysis. Documents 3 saturation thresholds (60, 120, 150+ webs), conversion rate drop from 18% to 11% after 30% multi-contact, 6-week competitive response cycle, 65% probability of price war above 40% penetration in 8 weeks. Identifies WhatsApp groups as negative-sentiment amplifiers (3x impact). Recommends operational differentiation over technological.

nodes.label: index.html
nodes.summary: Homepty Swarm SPA entry point. Title 'Homepty Swarm - Predict Everything'. Meta description: 'Social Media Opinion Simulation System'. Default language zh with localStorage locale override. Loads fonts Inter, JetBrains Mono, Noto Sans SC, Space Grotesk. Mounts Vue.js app via /src/main.js into #app div.

nodes.label: Simulation Report fd139afc5936
nodes.summary: Market simulation report analyzing the Saltillo, Mexico real estate digital market. Composed of at least 3 sections covering financial context, advisor behavior/sales friction, and competitive saturation/response. Targets 180 real estate websites in a 3-month horizon.

nodes.label: Saltillo Real Estate Digital Market
nodes.summary: The target market for the 180-web sales goal. Composed of boutique agencies (40%), established agencies (35%), and independent advisors (25%). Market has high informal connectivity via WhatsApp groups. A 6-week critical window exists before competitive saturation and seasonal slowdown. Saturation thresholds at 60, 120, and 150+ webs.

nodes.label: 180 Webs Sales Goal
nodes.summary: Strategic objective to sell 180 real estate websites in Saltillo within 3 months. Economically viable but dependent on accelerating agency decision cycles. Risk factors: seasonal slowdown in month 3, competitive saturation, price wars above 40% market penetration.

nodes.label: Boutique Agencies Segment
nodes.summary: 40% of Saltillo real estate market potential. Decision timeline 2-3 weeks. Higher propensity to innovate for differentiation. More agile in digital adoption.

nodes.label: Established Agencies Segment
nodes.summary: 35% of Saltillo real estate market potential. Decision timeline 4-6 weeks. Require local case studies as validation. Respond defensively when >10% of client base is contacted by alternatives. Prefer performance guarantees and exit clauses over pricing discussions.

nodes.label: Independent Advisors Segment
nodes.summary: 25% of Saltillo real estate market potential. Fastest decision timeline 1-2 weeks. Sensitive to flexible payment models. Ask about payment options on first contact. Primary targets for new market entrants using commission-based pricing.

nodes.label: Sales Friction Points
nodes.summary: Four critical funnel drop-off points identified in simulation: price objection (60% of initial conversations), reference delay/unmet (45% abandon without verifiable case studies within 48h), decision delay (35% cool off after 2 weeks without active follow-up), internal competition (20% postpone to develop internal solutions). Abandonment peaks at days 10-15 of sales cycle.

nodes.label: Competitive Saturation Dynamics
nodes.summary: Market saturation model with 3 thresholds: 60 webs (high receptivity), 120 webs (active competitor comparison, +25% price objections), 150+ webs (demands unique differentiators). Conversion rate drops from 18% to 11% after 30% multi-contact. 6-week competitive response cycle from threat detection to countermeasures. Established competitors reduce prices 15-20% when threatened. 65% probability of price war if >40% penetration in 8 weeks.

nodes.label: Homepty Swarm System
nodes.summary: Social Media Opinion Simulation System with tagline 'Predict Everything'. Multi-language SPA (default zh, localStorage locale override). Vue.js frontend. Used for market simulation and report generation for real estate projects.

nodes.label: WhatsApp Network Effect in Saltillo
nodes.summary: Informal advisor WhatsApp groups act as market perception amplifiers. Negative word-of-mouth has 3x more impact than positive on adoption decisions. A single negative experience shared can neutralize up to 15 subsequent positive commercial contacts. Agencies share provider information more frequently than expected.

nodes.label: Senior Advisor Technology Resistance
nodes.summary: Advisors with 10+ years experience (~40% of target market) show specific resistance patterns: request extended trials (30+ days), require in-person training, prefer references from same age group, distrust automation that may replace their judgment. Core insight: they reject the perception of being made dispensable, not technology itself.
